fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Louisiana Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer physical therapist license to louisiana, physical therapist compact louisiana, louisiana physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana Board of Physical Therapy Examiners

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Louisiana.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Physical Therapy Program
steps.description: Complete a physical therapy program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation in Physical Therapy Education (CAPTE).

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Successfully pass the NPTE for Physical Therapists, administered by the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT).

steps.title: Apply for a Louisiana Physical Therapist License
steps.description: Submit an application to the Louisiana Board of Physical Therapy Examiners, including all required documentation and fees, and complete a criminal background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Louisiana physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Louisiana participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Louisiana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Louisiana
hero.dek: Louisiana Physical Therapist License requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the National Physical Therapy Examination, and a criminal background check.
